flashcards.definition: Both momentum and kinetic energy are conserved; objects bounce apart.
Momentum is conserved but kinetic energy is not; some KE is lost to heat/deformation.
Objects stick together after collision; maximum KE loss.
e = (separation speed) / (approach speed); e = 1 elastic, e = 0 perfectly inelastic.
In 2D, conserve momentum separately in x and y directions.
